resources.string: For the needs of this research project, AmIUnique collects anonymous data about the configuration of smartphones, operating systems, browsers and plugins. If you click the "Scan my device fingerprint" button, this type of information will be collected from this application. Although these kinds of data may form a "device fingerprint" that could in principle be combined with information about page requests and identifying details in order to track people\'s browsing habits, we will never do so.

We may publish or share aggregated, statistical data from the AmIUnique project in order to facilitate privacy research, educate people about privacy problems, and to aid in the development of privacy-enhancing technologies. We have gone to great lengths to ensure that AmIUnique does not produce any records about anyone\'s browsing habits or the identities of any individual visitors, so we will never be in a position where we could share any such records. AmIUnique has no Third-Party Service Providers.
Our Use of Information from AmIUnique

We will analyze the collected data in order to establish if smartphone fingerprints can be used as a means to track online users. The purpose of this analysis is to set an automatic procedure to identify the most revealing features to inform platform holders to fix them to better improve privacy.

In a general way, Inria commits to carry out technical and organizational means to protect all information we gathered against illegal or fortuitous destruction, fortuitous loss, alteration, diffusion or unauthorized access. Nevertheless, Inria shall be required to divulge any information to comply with any applicable law or rules, or to respond to any administrative or judiciary order.\n\n Although we make good faith efforts to store information collected in a secure operating environment, we cannot guarantee complete security. Information collected will be maintained for a length of time appropriate to the needs of this research project.
This application was created and is maintained by a team of researchers, who investigates the software diversity on the web. \nThe app is developed by the team behind the AmIUnique.org website.
What is the purpose of this web site?
Inform users about their position within the web ecosystem

With this app, we aim at providing users with some basic information about their configuration and how trackable it is. We also want to exploit the data we collect to advise platform holders about how they can make their operating systems less trackable.
Investigate the diversity of device fingerprints

The research question we investigate is: how can we automatically reconfigure a user\'s platform to fuzz the device fingerprint in a realistic manner? The degree of realism is important to prevent users of our solution to be detected as such. The collection of a large base of real device fingerprints will allow us to tune our solution towards platforms that are as different as possible yet not distinguishable as "strange" configurations.

This application aims at studying the diversity of smartphone fingerprints and providing developers with data to help them design good defenses. This application collects anonymous data without using a single permission (apart from the one providing access to the Internet) related to the device\'s configuration and operating system. The goal of our research is to understand if any application on the Play Store could identify users without relying on any given identifiers already present on Android.
If you consent and click on the button "Scan my device fingerprint", we will collect your device fingerprint.

string.name: alert_details
string.u: More details are available in the Privacy Policy.

resources.string: Regularly scanning your fingerprint helps track its evolution. Enable notifications to receive a weekly reminder.

string.name: alert_notification_details
string.u: To change the frequency or time, go to Settings.

resources.string: Receive a weekly notification to remind you to rescan your device fingerprint
How often to repeat the reminder
Pick time when to trigger the notification
It\'s time to rescan your device fingerprint!
I consent to the collection of my device fingerprint
I consent to receive a weekly reminder notification to rescan my fingerprint
To request the removal of your fingerprint, please send us an email with your identifier included.
